name: TitleMax Title Loans
description_short: TitleMax provides car and motorcycle title loans and pawns, offering same-day cash using your vehicle title as collateral. No credit check required; APR typically 100–300%.
description_long:
TitleMax was founded in 1998 and has grown into one of the largest title lending companies in the United States, operating 900–1,100+ locations across approximately 14–16 states. Historically headquartered in Savannah, Georgia, the company now maintains corporate offices in Dallas, Texas. It is privately held under TMX Finance LLC, which was acquired by Community Choice Financial (CCF Holdings), a parent umbrella that also includes sister brands TitleBucks, EquityAuto Loan, and InstaLoan. TitleMax holds no CDFI certification, HUD approval, or NFCC membership — it is a for-profit, high-cost lender, not a nonprofit financial services organization.

TitleMax's core products are car title loans, car title pawns, motorcycle title loans, and motorcycle title pawns. The model is straightforward: a borrower brings in a vehicle they own outright (or nearly so), submits the title as collateral, and receives a cash loan — typically on the same day, in a single store visit. No credit check is required; loan amounts are determined primarily by vehicle value and state law, with up to $25,000 available in certain markets. Default repayment terms are 30 days, though TitleMax accommodates weekly, bi-weekly, and monthly schedules aligned to borrowers' pay cycles. The borrower retains possession and full use of the vehicle during the entire loan term. In select states, TitleMax also offers unsecured personal loans (signature loans). APR runs approximately 100%–300% annually — consistent with national title lending averages cited by the FTC.

TitleMax's principal differentiator is scale and speed. With 900+ physical locations, same-day in-store funding, and no credit check requirement, it delivers immediate capital to consumers who cannot qualify through traditional channels. Its online account portal (account.titlemax.com) and mobile app allow existing borrowers to manage accounts digitally. The company is particularly dominant in Georgia, where its title pawn model is widely used. Early payoff is permitted in most states without prepayment penalties, giving disciplined borrowers flexibility to reduce total interest cost.

TitleMax's primary strength is accessibility — it fills a genuine gap for vehicle owners with poor or no credit who need cash quickly and have no other viable options. However, the cost of that access is substantial: at 100%–300% APR, borrowing costs can be severe, and defaulting means losing the vehicle used as collateral. The company has faced significant federal regulatory action: the CFPB fined TitleMax $9 million in 2016 for unlawful debt collection and loan renewal practices, and in 2023, parent company TMX Finance was ordered to pay $15 million ($10M penalty plus $5M consumer relief) for systematically violating the Military Lending Act — extending illegal loans to active-duty service members and their families at nearly triple the 36% MLA cap, and altering borrower records to conceal military status. Consumers should treat TitleMax as a last resort, enter with a concrete repayment plan, and fully understand the total cost before signing.
address: 2717 E Belt Line Rd Apt 100, Carrollton, TX 75006

pricing.guarantee_details: Not applicable — lending product. No money-back guarantee exists for title loans or pawns.

pros: Same-day cash disbursement — loan funded in a single in-store visit with no waiting period
No credit check required — eligibility based on vehicle value, not credit score or history
Borrow up to $25,000 depending on vehicle value and state-specific regulations
Keep and drive your vehicle throughout the entire loan term — no surrender required
900–1,100+ physical store locations across 14–16 states for in-person access
Flexible repayment schedules — weekly, bi-weekly, or monthly aligned to borrower pay cycle
Early payoff permitted in most states without prepayment penalties
cons: APR typically 100%–300% annually — among the highest borrowing costs of any legal consumer lending product in the U.S.
Vehicle repossession is the direct consequence of default — borrowers risk losing their primary transportation
CFPB fined $9 million in 2016 for unlawful debt collection and illegal loan renewal practices
CFPB ordered $15 million penalty in 2023 for violating the Military Lending Act by lending to active-duty military families at nearly 3x the 36% legal cap and falsifying borrower records
Available in only approximately 14–16 states — inaccessible to the majority of U.S. consumers
best_for: Vehicle owners with poor or no credit who own a paid-off car or motorcycle and need fast cash
Borrowers who need same-day funding and cannot qualify for traditional bank loans or credit cards
Consumers needing larger emergency amounts beyond what payday lenders offer, up to $25,000
Non-military individuals in TitleMax-operating states who fully understand and accept high-cost secured lending terms
diagnosis: TitleMax is genuinely useful only for vehicle owners with poor or no credit who have exhausted lower-cost alternatives and need fast, same-day cash — the 100%–300% APR makes this a costly last resort, not a routine borrowing tool. Active-duty military members and their dependents are legally prohibited from using TitleMax under the Military Lending Act, a restriction the company was fined $15 million for violating in 2023. Borrowers must have a concrete repayment plan before applying, as defaulting results in direct vehicle repossession.
services: Personal loans (unsecured signature loans, select states only)
Mobile app for account access and management
Flexible repayment scheduling (weekly, bi-weekly, or monthly)
Early loan payoff without prepayment penalty (most states)
